AoE Workshop Questions, Week 4



Module 8: Product Launch Series (PLS)

Karma Lodro Senge

This may have been covered already, so please excuse it if it has.

If you have people going through your VNL, SOS's, and maybe even in a SPP, but at the same time you are also running a PLS, do you care that the person is getting the SPP and the PLS at the same time? Will this not cause some confusion? How can we do this efficiently?

Ian Chew

Hi guys, I’m loving AOE so much (been through ARM, but this is the first time I’m applying the knowledge a lot more intentionally.)

No questions re: how to implement, especially since this isn’t a cookie-cutter program — I’m adapting what you said based on my context. 

My big questions for now are around mindset and mastery, especially when it comes to PLS… (doing a product launch this month)

1. Mindset

I can’t help but marvel at your conversion rates (10%++). It’s inspiring and makes me want to double down on applying your teachings (I know that it will take time to get there!) At the same time, it’s discouraging in the short term… for our most recent PLS, we sold to a list of 900 customers and our conversion rate is 1.66%. A far cry from the 10%... 

More deets as below:  

List size: 900

Conversion: 1.66%

Opt out rate: 0.1-0.2% 

Complaint rate: 0.00% 



Email

Open %

Click %

#1

56.6%

11.7%

#2

60.4%

12.3%

#3

59.4%

10.7%

#4

60.4%

6.7%

#5

58.6%

12.2%



How I’m reframing it on my end:

It will take time to iterate, since it’s technically the first time we’re testing message-market fit/product-market fit (we did a beta test launch of the same time back in Feb, but we changed the audience targeting and corresponding substantially for this launch) 

Most of the sales will come after 90 days i.e. sometime after the launch. We should sell it again (and we are making plans to do so in 2022 Q1).

Any other mindset advice you have for me — so that I continue to support our customers whether they purchase or not?

Module 11: Advanced Stuff

Other questions

Denise Schmeichler

I have written an SOS series that I would like to also implement as a video course with the idea of offering it as a free course that does not require registration.

My plan is to use the text SOS series as part of my worldbuilding, people that find me organically would be exposed to this content.

At the same time, I would like to use the sequence in my email follow up and for those who want to go even deeper down the rabbit hole I would like to include a link that takes them to this SOS video series which I am thinking would be very similar to the written version but in video.

I want to give people the option to consume my content in video if they prefer to and also I am thinking that it will be a completely different experience for the person watching the video than reading.

I have a master class that helps book me discovery calls.

And my goal is for people to register for my master class so that they can book a call with me. So my objective with the video course is to send people to the master class.

I am thinking that creating this type of content with video is going to help create trust and authority and I was wondering if you have applied this idea of worldbuilding to video or have seen others do it successfully.

Follow-up

I have a follow up to my question, if it's possible to add to this question:

People enter my world by signing up for the free masterclass, that's how they start receiving my emails.

Until now, I have been sending the same emails to the people who attend the masterclass than to those that do not attend it.

Would you recommend 2 different RBS sequences?

What is your strategy to unsubscribe people, I am referring about the people that don't open the emails, I am guessing that the ones do open them stay until they decide it's not for them.

Kim Lange

I'd love to hear more on how you think about creating your manifestos and where they fit into the AoE. Thank you!

Ian Chew

Mastery 

This is more of a general question: What’s your deliberate practice routine like? How do you become world-class at email copywriting? How many years would it take, you reckon?

(I’ve been putting in the time and effort to learn and practice. For one, I started to track email writing time, and also make sure I use a checklist each time I write. Not looking for shortcuts — just your perspective on your own mastery journeys as fellow marketers and craftspeople.)



Ryan Coisson

I'm curious if you know a way to use custom fields in Convert Kit or another feature to hardcode in someone's affiliate ID for Clickbank.

Basically what I'm wanting to do is if someone promotes my program as an affiliate the opt-ins have their affiliate ID hardcoded in so if someone buys say 5 months later the affiliate ID is reapplied anytime the click an applicable link.

Any idea on how to execute this?
